title: Ensure no duplicate group names exist

prose.en: Ensure no duplicate group names exist

description.en: Although the `groupadd` program will not let you create a duplicate group name, it is possible for an administrator to manually edit the `/etc/group` file and change the group name.

rationale.en: If a group is assigned a duplicate group name, it will create and have access to files with the first GID for that group in `/etc/group` . Effectively, the GID is shared, which is a security problem.

audit.en: Based on the results of the audit script, establish unique names for the user groups. File group ownerships will automatically reflect the change as long as the groups have unique GIDs.

remediation.en: Based on the results of the audit script, establish unique names for the user groups. File group ownerships will automatically reflect the change as long as the groups have unique GIDs.

references: NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5: CM-1, CM-2, CM-6, CM-7, IA-5
